tax: Placed at genus rank, Sleetling is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a hail-forming cloud body. A new species belongs here only if it shows a supercooled cloud body.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a swirling grey hail-cloud 1.5 to 2.5 m across, denser than ordinary cave mist, that bulges before each volley. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — melting hailstones, ice-pocked stone, and a sudden chill that frosts breath before any cloud is seen — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Sleetling occupies cold updraft shafts. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where water-pressure chills an updraft and recur with the updraft re-chills, sustaining themselves on chilled updraft air.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: flings hail volleys at intruders, chills the air to numb pursuers, and thickens to dim lights. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: a supercooled cloud body, air-motion and warmth sensing, cold-immunity, and a defining warmth-failure. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: hail-shot and air-chilling. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Sleetling matters because ranged ice hazard in cold shafts. It lets survey reports sort hail-cloud forms from creeping-rime ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Sleetling is the genus-rank grouping of hail-flinging cloud elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a supercooled cloud body that flings stinging hail, and divide below into species groups by updraft chill and hail size. A cloud that hurls ice; the only Water genus that attacks at range.
